title: Accessible Puzzle Interface and Multichannel Feedback · Language Clinic: Accessibility Evidence
summary: Walls, visited cells, current focus, route, and errors are communicated only by color and animation, while the Canvas has no usable alternative. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the accessibility evidence increment in keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.
Offer pause, instant mode, adjustable speed, reduced motion, hints, route reveal, and difficulty dimensions without penalty.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Predict Accessibility Evidence · Theory Accessibility Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory puzzle team handles case 3965 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-accessible-interface-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-accessible-interface-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_accessible_interface_3(essential_states: int = 9, text_equivalents: int = 9, keyboard_actions: int = 9, color_only_states: int = 0, open_barriers: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0:
        return False, text_equivalents, "accessibility-count"
    if text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states:
        return False, text_equivalents, "equivalent-experience"
    if color_only_states > 0:
        return False, color_only_states, "color-only-cue"
    if open_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_barriers, "unclosed-barrier"
    assert text_equivalents == essential_states
    return True, text_equivalents, "multichannel-maze-interface"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Build Accessibility Evidence evidence · Theory Accessibility Evid…
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-accessibility-evidence-1j4acn" that uses case 5688 to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Build Accessibility Evidence evidence · Theory Accessibility Evid….
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Accessibility Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Build Accessibility Evidence evidence · Theory Accessibility Evid…, keep Accessibility Evidence connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-accessibility-evidence-1j4acn
# Competency: maze-accessibility-evidence.
# Case 5688: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_accessibility_evidence_1j4acn(essential_states: int = 9, text_equivalents: int = 9, keyboard_actions: int = 9, color_only_states: int = 0, open_barriers: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1j4acn = "1j4acn-nca4j1-5688"
    assert evidence_variant_1j4acn
    evidence_axes_1j4acn = (
        "grid1j4acn",
        "topology5688",
        "searchnca4j1",
        "event1j4acn5688",
        "access56881j4acn",
        "recoverynca4j15688",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1j4acn)) == 6
    if essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0:
        return False, text_equivalents, "accessibility-count"
    if text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states:
        return False, text_equivalents, "equivalent-experience"
    if color_only_states > 0:
        return False, color_only_states, "color-only-cue"
    if open_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_barriers, "unclosed-barrier"
    assert text_equivalents == essential_states
    return True, text_equivalents, "multichannel-maze-interface"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessibility Evidence model · Theory Accessibility Ev…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Accessibility Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 8299 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessibility Evidence model · Theory Accessibility Ev…, keep Accessibility Evidence connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.
